text: in short the housing boom appears to have been driven by a combination of fundamentals government incentives and a degree of overshooting
llm_diagnosis: Abel triggers on 'boom' and 'overshooting' as overheating signals pushing toward tightening, but the explanatory/retrospective tone (attributing housing strength to incentives and fundamentals) is actually accommodative by not recommending action. Abel keyword-matched on 'inflation' direction ignoring the complaint framing.

text: many governmental services are provided at the state and local level
llm_diagnosis: A bland fiscal-structure description triggers Abel's default that 'government services' equals spending = dovish. Missed that the context in central-bank speech frames decentralization/federalism favorably (anti-expansion = hawkish). Over-flattens fiscal sentiment to spending-positive.

llm_diagnosis: No direct macro variable is mentioned; Abel's Markov blanket has no anchor so it defaults to 'legislators negotiating' = fiscal accommodation = dovish. Missing the implicit context (likely deficit-reduction compromise) that makes this hawkish.

llm_diagnosis: Abel anchors on 'high interest rates abroad' and routes through the federal-funds node to hawkish, but the sentence is critical/comparative: complaining that foreign reliance on tight policy yields high rates, implying the US view favors easier policy. text: nonetheless the attention paid the deficit  following the stock market crash means that fiscal stimulus will be less than in recent years
llm_diagnosis: Explicit statement that 'fiscal stimulus will be less' is hawkish, but Abel likely triggers on 'stock market crash' as a dovish shock signal and over-weights that crisis context over the fiscal-restraint conclusion. Present-state anchoring on 'crash' dominates.

llm_diagnosis: 'Improving economy' triggers growth-node dovish (rate-cut-friendly) framing, but the operative clause is 'deficit has been tamed' which signals fiscal discipline = hawkish. Abel weighted GDP-improvement edge over the deficit-reduction concept.

text: of course i do not view this weakness negatively because deficit reduction is long overdue

text: the coordination and expertise at the state level in these matters is an essential corrective

llm_diagnosis: Abel pulls 'correct the external imbalance' toward tightening/hawkish, but warnings of unsustainable external debt in this speech imply acceptance of easier policy tradeoff. 'Correct' + 'debt' keywords flip direction wrongly.

text: the tax cut bill of course is not the only source of potential fiscal stimulus over the next couple of years

text: as a result total federal outlays will rise without being reflected in the budget totals

llm_diagnosis: 'Prevent excessive growth of aggregate demand' pings hawkish in Abel, but the sentence's primary clause is checking decline (stimulus, dovish). Blanket weighted the future-tightening language over current-stimulus emphasis.
